channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Live: ASX to rise after tech rally on Wall Street
item.description: Oil prices have dropped again as investors hope for peace in the Middle East and an end to the global energy shock. Follow the day's events and insights as they happen with our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.
item.dc:creator: Emilia Terzon

media:group.media:description: Two women look on as the ASX suffers large falls on September 14, 2022.

item.title: 20 million illegal vapes seized by border force in just over two years
item.description: More than 20 million illegal vapes, with a street value of just over $1 billion, have been seized by law enforcement authorities since Australia's vaping reforms came into effect.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Carbonell and Caitlyn Gribbin

media:group.media:description: More than 19 million vaping products have been seized by Australian Border Force (ABF) since January 2024.

item.title: Controversial Sunshine Coast housing development gets federal approval
item.description: Up to 12,000 homes could be added to the fast-growing region after a major hurdle was cleared for the contentious Aura South development to proceed.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Ross, Madeline Grace, Alex Easton, and Sarah Howells

media:group.media:description: The Aura South development would be built alongside the existing Aura and Pelican Waters communities.

item.title: Australia's Kasatkina sets up French Open clash with top seed Sabalenka
item.description: Daria Kasatkina will face world number one Aryna Sabalenka in the third round as fellow Australian Kimberly Birrell departs the singles draw in Paris.

media:group.media:description: Daria Kasatkina will meet Aryna Sabalenka in the third round.

item.title: Darwin's Middle Arm precinct could be roadblocked by endangered herb
item.description: The NT government is arguing its planned mega Middle Arm gas industry precinct will be needed to help shore up Australia's future fuel and energy supplies — but it has just identified that a tiny endangered herb could be growing in the way.
item.dc:creator: Jane Bardon

media:group.media:description: An environmental impact report for the Middle Arm project says it could threaten several endangered species, including this small herb.

item.title: Live: Australia secures first vessel for strategic fleet
item.description: Australia has secured the first vessel for its maritime strategic fleet, which is to be comprised of up to 12 privately owned, commercially operated ships. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Boscaini

media:group.media:description: Infrastructure minister Catherine King said xxx

item.title: Kids protest against McDonald's after fast-food giant buys land in small town
item.description: Children turn out in protest after it's revealed the global fast-food giant has paid $4.6 million for a prominent site in central Victoria's Castlemaine.
item.dc:creator: Nate Woodall, Anna McGuinness, and Tyrone Dalton

media:group.media:description: Siblings Alby, 8, and 11-year-old Wren say they don't want a McDonald's in Castlemaine.

item.title: Canberra man jailed over sexual abuse of 'extremely vulnerable' child
item.description: A man who sexually assaulted a 12-year-old girl after a "chance encounter" at a Canberra shopping centre has been jailed, with the judge finding he took advantage of an "extremely vulnerable" child.
item.dc:creator: Zaarkacha Marlan

media:group.media:description: Laurence Martin Coghlan was sentenced to four years and two months' jail.

item.title: 'Stunning' weekend for south-east Queensland before cool change
item.description: Queensland residents can expect a reprieve from the recent "wet and stormy" weather this weekend, with cooler-than-average temperatures on the way.

media:group.media:description: A rainbow appears on the Gold Coast.

item.title: Qld government to push ahead with Victoria Park demolition amid heritage challenges
item.description: Excavators and fencing began appearing at the Brisbane park earlier this week, but the federal government has not yet assessed challenges under the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Heritage Protection Act.
item.dc:creator: Will Murray

media:group.media:description: Gaja Kerry Charlton is a signatory to five challenges to the Victoria Park stadium project.

item.title: Man devastated to lose home deposit in 'eco village' development
item.description: It was lauded as a solution to the housing crisis, but those involved say plans to build an "eco village" in northern New South Wales have gone awry.
item.dc:creator: Alex Turner-Cohen

media:group.media:description: Serge Killingbeck says he has been unable to recover his $36,000 down payment after a dispute with the developer.

item.title: French Open favourite loses to 56th-ranked amid dizzy spell
item.description: Jannik Sinner's French Open campaign is over after the world number one struggled with illness in a shock second-round defeat to Juan Manuel Cerúndolo.

media:group.media:description: Jannik Sinner was in discomfort throughout his five-set loss to Juan Manuel Cerúndolo.

item.title: Netanyahu directs Israel's military to take over 70 per cent of Gaza
item.description: Israel effectively controls an estimated 64 per cent of Gaza, now bombarded to ruins by Israel's two-year military assault that followed the 2023 Hamas attack on southern Israel.

media:group.media:description: Israel effectively controls an estimated 64 per cent of Gaza.

item.title: Celine Cremer searchers turn attention to missing man Peter Willoughby
item.description: A group of volunteers, which played a major role in finding the remains of a lost Belgian tourist earlier this year, will today begin searching for another missing person in Tasmania's wilderness.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith and Morgan Timms

media:group.media:description: Photo of missing Scotsdale man Peter Willoughby, provided by his family.

item.title: Major wine retailer puts vineyards up for sale amid industry-wide squeeze
item.description: Endeavour Group, which owns Dan Murphy's and BWS, announces it will step away from some key vineyards and wineries in South Australia, Victoria and Tasmania, and close a major South Australian bottling plant.
item.dc:creator: Elsie Adamo and Selina Green

media:group.media:description: The wine industry has been in a state of transition after a worldwide oversupply of wine in recent years.

item.title: Officeworks to offshore hundreds of jobs in search for white collar workers
item.description: The Australian office products retailer is planning to offshore hundreds of jobs to India and the Philippines, joining a growing trend of businesses hoping to find more highly-skilled white collar workers overseas.
item.dc:creator: Isobel Roe

media:group.media:description: Dozens of workers at the company's Western Sydney customer service centre have been made redundant.

item.title: Letter alleges 'unlawful conspiracy' to oust new APY Lands general manager
item.description: The new manager of a remote South Australian Aboriginal land corporation has alleged there is an "unlawful conspiracy" to oust him, but the board says his position has already been terminated.
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Bermingham

media:group.media:description: Trent Wilkinson was appointed as general manager of the APY Lands in December.

item.title: Patients demand fix to 'unacceptable' travel subsidy scheme
item.description: Kevin O’Neill says Queensland's patient travel subsidy scheme left him stranded with a dangerous medical condition 300 kilometres from home. Patient advocates say it is one of a litany of failures they hope will be fixed in a review of the scheme.
item.dc:creator: James C Taylor

media:group.media:description: Kevin and Rebecca O'Neill (left and right) are calling for increased rates and improved processing times for their travel subsidy claims.

item.title: 'Am I safe?' Return of 'ISIS brides' sparks fear in Yazidi communities
item.description: The return of several 'ISIS brides' from Syria has been re-traumatising for Australian Yazidis, many of whom escaped sexual slavery at the hands of the terrorist organisation.
item.dc:creator: Marina Trajkovich and Jostina Basta

media:group.media:description: Amera Ali moved to Australia after escaping sexual slavery in Syria.

item.title: Aussies facing 'most important day' of World Championship campaign
item.description: The threat of relegation hangs over the Australian men's rugby sevens squad ahead of the final two rounds of the World Championship series.
item.dc:creator: Luke Pentony

media:group.media:description: Australia skipper Henry Hutchison is not looking too far ahead in the World Championship.

item.title: 'Deeply disappointed': Premier silent on leaked email complaint
item.description: Ruth Forrest says she is "deeply disappointed" about a lack of government response over a leaked email and is considering referring a complaint to WorkSafe Tasmania and parliament's Privileges Committee.
item.dc:creator: Jano Gibson

media:group.media:description: Ruth Forrest says she had still not received a response from the government about the leaked email on Thursday night.

item.title: 'People do care': Why private owners are handing land back
item.description: It has been two decades since substantial land hand-backs in Tasmania, so private owners are doing it instead. Near Hobart, a piece of land that was once a dumping ground is being restored.
item.dc:creator: Adam Holmes

media:group.media:description: The property in Collinsvale will be used to train the next generation of rangers in land management.

item.title: Need for mine's hybrid power plant 'embarrassing', outback MP says
item.description: An international energy company is building Australia's largest off-grid renewable hybrid power facility, as a state politician criticises the need for it.
item.dc:creator: Abbey Halter

media:group.media:description: At the end of its construction, the power facility at Eva Copper Mine will be about the size of 53 Melbourne Cricket Grounds.

item.title: Austrian man jailed for Taylor Swift concert terror plot
item.description: A man who plotted but ultimately failed to launch a terrorist attack at Taylor Swift concert in Vienna, forcing the pop star to cancel three performances, is sentenced to 15 years behind bars.

media:group.media:description: The man had planned to attack a Taylor Swift concert in Vienna nearly two years ago.

item.title: Labor campaigned on 'integrity' so the NACC should be enjoying public trust by now
item.description: In an extraordinary irony, the National Anti-Corruption Commission has become best known for "own goals", rocked by internal scandals and attracting a litany of complaints.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Grattan

media:group.media:description: Outgoing NACC chief Paul Brereton believes in some ways he contributed to his own "downfall".
